title: Room Divider Closet
summary: A freestanding double-tower closet with paneled sides and a hanging rod, built to divide a room while providing full closet storage from either side.
description:
This is a genuinely different kind of closet than this catalog's wall-hung modular system: instead of hanging off a level cleat, it's a self-supporting freestanding unit meant to sit in the middle of a room, splitting it in two while offering hanging and shelf storage. Two identical plywood towers, joined at the back with 2x4s, form the structural core; wall paneling wraps the outside faces so the divider looks finished from the room side, not just the closet side.

Build the two towers first with your joinery of choice — pocket holes work well, two screws per joint — then join them together with 2x4s screwed flush to the back, which is what turns two separate towers into one rigid divider unit. Attach the paneling to the outward faces next, nailed to the 2x4s and the towers' back edges, followed by 1x4 top/bottom trim and 1x3 vertical trim that dresses up every visible seam.

The closet rod hangs on the inside, between the towers, giving the unit real hanging capacity rather than just shelving. Because this divider is freestanding rather than anchored to studs, keep the overall footprint and loaded weight in mind — a very tall, narrow-footprint version of this build is more tip-prone than the source's proportions, so don't stretch the height significantly beyond what's shown here without adding a base footprint to match.

tools.note: Ripping the plywood into tower sides.

materials.name: 3/4" plywood, sheets
materials.note: Ripped into 11-3/4" wide strips for tower sides and shelves.

cutList.note: Cut to fit ceiling height minus at least 1/2" for clearance.

steps.title: Build the two towers
steps.body: Build two identical towers from the sides and shelves, using pocket holes and two 1-1/4" screws per joint.

steps.title: Join the towers
steps.body: Attach the two towers together with the 2x4 back connectors, flush to the back side, using 2-1/2" self-tapping screws.

steps.title: Attach the paneling
steps.body: Attach the wall paneling to the 2x4s and the back edges of the towers with panel nails or fasteners suited to your materials.

steps.title: Add the trim and closet rod
steps.body: Cut and nail the top and bottom trim first with 1-1/4" brad nails. Measure and cut the vertical trim pieces to fit and nail to the 2x4s and tower back edges. Hang the closet rod on the inside between the towers.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand any rough edges, then prime and paint.

unresolvedImages.alt: Freestanding double-tower closet used as a room divider
